# Review: deepmind/914.lean ‚ÄĒ ErdŇĎs Problem 914

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line (`import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`), immediately after the copyright block, separated by one blank line.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 914`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/914`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (minimum-degree condition forcing vertex-disjoint copies of $K_r$).
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[HaSz70]` Hajnal and Szemer√©di (1970).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos914 ... end Erdos914`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos914`).
- [x] `open SimpleGraph Finset` appears **before** the namespace block (line 36).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- N/A ‚ÄĒ no `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_914` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` appear on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`) describing the result.
- [x] Docstring references the source paper (`[HaSz70]`).

### `answer()` usage

The problem is a direct assertion: "Every graph with $r \cdot m$ vertices and minimum degree at least $m(r-1)$ contains $m$ vertex-disjoint copies of $K_r$." This is **Case 3** ‚ÄĒ no `answer()` wrapper needed.

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper) ‚ÄĒ correct for a direct assertion.
- [x] No quantifiers lifted into theorem parameters that should be after `answer()` ‚ÄĒ not applicable (Case 3, no `answer()`).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research solved` ‚ÄĒ valid category. The Hajnal‚ÄďSzemer√©di theorem is a proven result but not formally verified in Lean, so `research solved` is correct.
- [x] Not marked `research open` (theorem has `sorry` only because the proof is not yet formalized, which is expected for `research solved`).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5` (Combinatorics).
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order (trivially, only one number).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code. Appropriate for a graph-theoretic/combinatorial result.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by` with `sorry` on the next line (multi-line format, acceptable per checklist ¬ß9).
- [x] No `sorry` in any `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations (none exist).
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A ‚ÄĒ no variants present. The file formalizes a single theorem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout (4-space continuation for multi-line theorem parameters is standard Lean style).
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚Č•`, `‚ąÄ`, `‚ąÉ`, `‚Č†`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (<100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run in this review (requires project build environment).
- [ ] Custom linter pass not verified in this review.

## Mathematical Correctness Notes

The formalization faithfully captures the Hajnal‚ÄďSzemer√©di theorem:
- The graph has `r * m` vertices (modeled over `Fin (r * m)`).
- Minimum degree condition: `‚ąÄ v, G.degree v ‚Č• m * (r - 1)` ‚ÄĒ correctly avoids natural number underflow since `r ‚Č• 2`.
- Conclusion: existence of `m` subsets (`Fin m ‚Üí Finset`), each of size `r`, each a clique in `G`, pairwise vertex-disjoint. This correctly encodes "$m$ vertex-disjoint copies of $K_r$."

## Verdict

**PASS** ‚ÄĒ all applicable checklist items are satisfied. The file is clean, well-documented, mathematically faithful, and follows all style conventions. The only unchecked items are build verification (¬ß12), which requires running in the project environment.

    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 914
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/914](https://www.erdosproblems.com/914)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $r \geq 2$ and $m \geq 1$. Every graph with $r \cdot m$ vertices and minimum degree at
    25‚Üíleast $m \cdot (r-1)$ contains $m$ vertex-disjoint copies of $K_r$.
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíWhen $r = 2$ this follows from Dirac's theorem. Corr√°di and Hajnal proved this when $r = 3$.
    28‚ÜíHajnal and Szemer√©di proved this for all $r \geq 4$.
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíThis is known as the Hajnal‚ÄďSzemer√©di theorem.
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí[HaSz70] Hajnal, A. and Szemer√©di, E., _Proof of a conjecture of P. ErdŇĎs_. Combinatorial theory
    33‚Üíand its applications, II (Proc. Colloq., Balatonf√ľred, 1969) (1970), 601-623.
    34‚Üí-/
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Finset
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üínamespace Erdos914
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/--
    41‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 914 (proved by Hajnal and Szemer√©di [HaSz70]):
    42‚Üí
    43‚ÜíLet $r \geq 2$ and $m \geq 1$. Every graph on $r \cdot m$ vertices with minimum degree at
    44‚Üíleast $m \cdot (r-1)$ contains $m$ vertex-disjoint copies of $K_r$ (complete graphs
    45‚Üíon $r$ vertices).
    46‚Üí-/
    47‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    48‚Üítheorem erdos_914 (r m : ‚Ąē) (hr : r ‚Č• 2) (hm : m ‚Č• 1)
    49‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph (Fin (r * m))) [DecidableRel G.Adj]
    50‚Üí    (hdeg : ‚ąÄ v : Fin (r * m), G.degree v ‚Č• m * (r - 1)) :
    51‚Üí    ‚ąÉ S : Fin m ‚Üí Finset (Fin (r * m)),
    52‚Üí      (‚ąÄ i, (S i).card = r) ‚ąß
    53‚Üí      (‚ąÄ i, G.IsClique (S i : Set (Fin (r * m)))) ‚ąß
    54‚Üí      (‚ąÄ i j, i ‚Č† j ‚Üí Disjoint (S i) (S j)) := by
    55‚Üí  sorry
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üíend Erdos914
    58‚Üí

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 914

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/914](https://www.erdosproblems.com/914)

Let $r \geq 2$ and $m \geq 1$. Every graph with $r \cdot m$ vertices and minimum degree at
least $m \cdot (r-1)$ contains $m$ vertex-disjoint copies of $K_r$.

When $r = 2$ this follows from Dirac's theorem. Corr√°di and Hajnal proved this when $r = 3$.
Hajnal and Szemer√©di proved this for all $r \geq 4$.

This is known as the Hajnal‚ÄďSzemer√©di theorem.

[HaSz70] Hajnal, A. and Szemer√©di, E., _Proof of a conjecture of P. ErdŇĎs_. Combinatorial theory
and its applications, II (Proc. Colloq., Balatonf√ľred, 1969) (1970), 601-623.
-/

open SimpleGraph Finset

namespace Erdos914

/--
ErdŇĎs Problem 914 (proved by Hajnal and Szemer√©di [HaSz70]):

Let $r \geq 2$ and $m \geq 1$. Every graph on $r \cdot m$ vertices with minimum degree at
least $m \cdot (r-1)$ contains $m$ vertex-disjoint copies of $K_r$ (complete graphs
on $r$ vertices).
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_914 (r m : ‚Ąē) (hr : r ‚Č• 2) (hm : m ‚Č• 1)
    (G : SimpleGraph (Fin (r * m))) [DecidableRel G.Adj]
    (hdeg : ‚ąÄ v : Fin (r * m), G.degree v ‚Č• m * (r - 1)) :
    ‚ąÉ S : Fin m ‚Üí Finset (Fin (r * m)),
      (‚ąÄ i, (S i).card = r) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ i, G.IsClique (S i : Set (Fin (r * m)))) ‚ąß
      (‚ąÄ i j, i ‚Č† j ‚Üí Disjoint (S i) (S j)) := by
  sorry

end Erdos914

content.text: The review verdict is **PASS**. No changes needed.
